message:
feat(wezterm): Cx-first pane_tiered_scrollback_summary + get_text (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Extends WeztermHandle Cx-first coverage to two more mux-pool-backed
methods:

  - `pane_tiered_scrollback_summary_with_cx` (tiered scrollback
    telemetry read)
  - `get_text_with_cx` (full pane scrollback read, chunked)

Both use `pool.get_pane_render_changes_with_cx(cx)` and (for
get_text) `pool.get_lines_with_cx(cx)` for per-chunk reads. Caller
cancellation propagates directly through to the underlying
DirectMuxClient. On escapes=true, get_text_with_cx falls back to
the legacy CLI path (mux backend doesn't support escape extraction).

Scope:

1. crates/frankenterm-core/src/wezterm.rs (+135 lines)

   a. `WeztermClient::pane_tiered_scrollback_summary_with_cx(cx, pane_id)`

      Mirrors the legacy body with `pool.get_pane_render_changes_with_cx`.
      Mux circuit-breaker guard + error accounting preserved identically.
      Tiered scrollback is vendored-mux-only; CLI fallback surfaces the
      same CommandFailed error as the legacy path. Two cfg-gated
      variants (vendored+unix+asupersync + stub fallback).

   b. `WeztermClient::get_text_with_cx(cx, pane_id, escapes)`

      Full mux-pool fast path with Cx threaded through both
      `pool.get_pane_render_changes_with_cx` (for scrollback bounds)
      and `pool.get_lines_with_cx` (per-chunk scrollback fetch).
      On a long pane with many 2000-row chunks, caller cancellation
      now terminates promptly after the in-flight chunk instead of
      consuming the full scrollback-fetch budget. Two cfg-gated
      variants.

   Legacy methods preserved; strictly additive.

Verification (CC=llvm clang, CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-rusticmaple-target):

  * cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib wezterm::: 160/160 pass
    (no new tests — covered by pool `_with_cx` LabRuntime tests
    via delegation)
  * cargo clippy -p frankenterm-core --lib -D warnings: clean
    (fixed doc-lazy-continuation in the multi-line comment)
  * rustfmt --check: clean

Leaves ft-xbnl0.2.3 still in_progress. WeztermHandle Cx-first
coverage now 11 of ~22 async methods. Remaining mux-pool-backed
candidates (get_pane_render_changes passthrough, various pane
lifecycle helpers) can follow the same pattern; CLI-only methods
(spawn, activate_pane, kill_pane, zoom_pane, split_pane) gain
marginal Cx value without structural retry-helper refactor.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
